content:
IVX HOLDINGS — VERCEL REMOVAL COMMAND CENTER

MISSION

Create a complete Owner-only module and live dashboard that allows the owner to monitor all 9 assigned IVX AI senior developers while they remove every Vercel dependency from IVX Holdings end to end.

This is an execution and migration task.

Do not copy Vercel proprietary code.

Replace every Vercel-dependent function with IVX-controlled infrastructure using approved services such as Render, AWS, Supabase, Redis, direct AI-provider APIs, and IVX-owned backend services.

Do not return only a plan.

Inspect, implement, test, migrate, deploy, verify, and show live evidence.

==================================================
MODULE NAME
==================================================

Create:

IVX VERCEL EXIT COMMAND CENTER

Owner-only navigation:

Admin
→ IVX Engineering
→ Vercel Exit Command Center

Owner authorization must be enforced server-side.

==================================================
PRIMARY OBJECTIVES
==================================================

1. Discover every Vercel dependency.
2. Assign the work across 9 IVX AI senior developers.
3. Replace all Vercel services safely.
4. Show each AI agent’s work live.
5. Prevent conflicting file changes.
6. Test the replacement architecture.
7. Deploy the replacement services.
8. Cut production traffic away from Vercel.
9. Verify zero runtime dependency on Vercel.
10. Keep a safe rollback until final acceptance.
11. Deliver a directly downloadable Android APK after migration.
12. Provide evidence for every completed item.

==================================================
9 IVX AI SENIOR DEVELOPER ROLES
==================================================

AI 1 — MIGRATION ARCHITECT

Owns:

- Complete dependency inventory
- Replacement architecture
- Migration sequence
- Service boundaries
- Risk register
- Cutover and rollback strategy
- Final architecture approval

AI 2 — AI GATEWAY DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Replace ai-gateway.vercel.sh
- Direct AI-provider integrations
- Model routing
- Provider authentication
- Retry and timeout logic
- Rate limiting
- Usage and cost tracking
- AI health verification

AI 3 — BACKEND API DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Replace Vercel functions and API routes
- Move serverless logic to IVX backend
- Middleware
- Authentication enforcement
- Health and readiness endpoints
- Trace IDs
- Structured errors

AI 4 — MOBILE AND WEB DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Remove Vercel URLs from Expo, Android, iOS and web
- Replace SDK imports
- Replace frontend environment references
- Update API clients
- Update upload and callback URLs
- Verify mobile and browser behavior

AI 5 — DATABASE AND SUPABASE DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Data dependency audit
- Migrations
- Database functions
- Realtime
- Storage references
- RLS
- Background jobs connected to database events
- Data-integrity verification

AI 6 — DEVOPS AND INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Render services
- AWS services
- Redis
- Workers
- Queues
- Load balancing
- Autoscaling
- CI/CD
- DNS migration
- Environment separation
- Rollback deployment

AI 7 — SECURITY AND IDENTITY DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Secret migration
- Token rotation
- Owner/Admin authorization
- MFA
- CORS
- CSP
- Rate limiting
- Webhook verification
- Audit logs
- Secret exposure scan

AI 8 — QA AND PERFORMANCE DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Unit tests
- Integration tests
- End-to-end tests
- Regression tests
- Load tests
- Realtime tests
- Failure recovery
- Android verification
- Web verification
- Release gate

AI 9 — CODE REVIEW, EVIDENCE AND CUTOVER DEVELOPER

Owns:

- Pull-request review
- Merge coordination
- Evidence ledger
- Commit verification
- Deployment verification
- Production comparison
- Vercel-zero scan
- Cutover approval
- Final certification

==================================================
DASHBOARD — EXECUTIVE VIEW
==================================================

At the top display:

- Migration status
- Overall completion percentage
- Current phase
- Vercel dependencies discovered
- Dependencies removed
- Dependencies remaining
- APIs migrated
- Environment variables migrated
- Services deployed
- Tests passed
- Tests failed
- Current production commit
- Current deployment ID
- Current rollback target
- Last production health result
- Vercel traffic percentage
- IVX infrastructure traffic percentage
- Estimated monthly cost before migration
- Estimated monthly cost after migration
- Active incidents
- Current blockers

Completion percentage must come from verified task records.

Do not use fabricated percentages.

==================================================
DASHBOARD — 9 AI LIVE WORK TABLE
==================================================

Display one row for every AI:

| AI | Role | Current Task | Status | Progress | Last Action | File | Test | Commit | Deployment | Blocker |

For each AI show:

- AI number
- Agent name
- Permanent role
- Current task
- Current status
- Real progress
- Start time
- Last activity
- Files reserved
- Files changed
- Tests executed
- Test result
- Last commit SHA
- Pull request
- Deployment ID
- Production verification
- Current blocker
- Next action
- Time working
- Tasks completed today
- Tasks failed today

Statuses:

- IDLE
- DISCOVERING
- ANALYZING
- IMPLEMENTING
- WAITING FOR REVIEW
- TESTING
- TEST FAILED
- TEST PASSED
- DEPLOYING
- DEPLOYMENT FAILED
- DEPLOYED
- PRODUCTION VERIFIED
- BLOCKED
- REJECTED

Only PRODUCTION VERIFIED counts as complete.

==================================================
DASHBOARD — MIGRATION INVENTORY
==================================================

Create a searchable inventory containing:

- Dependency ID
- Vercel service
- Dependency type
- Source file
- Line/reference
- Runtime environment
- Current purpose
- Replacement service
- Assigned AI
- Risk
- Migration status
- Test status
- Commit SHA
- Deployment ID
- Cutover status
- Rollback method
- Evidence

Dependency types must include:

- AI Gateway
- API route
- Serverless function
- Edge function
- Middleware
- Hosting
- DNS
- Environment variable
- Secret
- Cron
- Queue
- Webhook
- Analytics
- Logging
- Storage
- Image optimization
- Build process
- Deployment hook
- SDK
- Package dependency
- Domain
- Redirect
- Callback URL
- CORS origin
- Documentation reference

==================================================
PHASE 1 — COMPLETE VERCEL DISCOVERY
==================================================

Search the entire codebase, configuration, database, secrets metadata, deployment settings, mobile bundle and documentation for:

- vercel
- vercel.app
- vercel.sh
- ai-gateway.vercel.sh
- @vercel/
- VERCEL_
- NEXT_PUBLIC_
- deployment hooks
- Vercel API tokens
- Vercel project IDs
- Vercel team IDs
- Edge Runtime
- Vercel cron configuration
- vercel.json
- callback and redirect URLs
- CORS origins
- hardcoded Vercel domains
- generated mobile bundle references
- database records containing Vercel URLs
- Supabase functions calling Vercel
- GitHub Actions calling Vercel
- DNS records pointing to Vercel

Return the exact count and location of every dependency.

Do not remove anything until the replacement is identified.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — REPLACEMENT ARCHITECTURE
==================================================

Map every Vercel function to an IVX-controlled replacement.

Required target architecture:

GitHub
→ CI/CD
→ Render or AWS backend
→ Supabase database/realtime/storage
→ Redis
→ Queue and workers
→ Direct AI providers through IVX AI Gateway
→ CDN/media infrastructure
→ Monitoring and alerting

For every dependency define:

- Current implementation
- Target implementation
- Data migration required
- Secret migration required
- DNS change required
- Downtime risk
- Rollback procedure
- Assigned AI
- Acceptance test

==================================================
PHASE 3 — REPLACE VERCEL AI GATEWAY
==================================================

Replace:

ai-gateway.vercel.sh

Create an IVX-owned AI Gateway endpoint such as:

POST /api/ivx/ai/chat
POST /api/ivx/ai/stream
GET /api/ivx/ai/models
GET /api/ivx/ai/health
GET /api/ivx/ai/usage
GET /api/ivx/ai/request/:traceId/status

Requirements:

- Direct provider authentication
- No Vercel proxy
- Provider failover
- Model routing
- Timeouts
- Retries
- Rate limiting
- Idempotency
- Trace IDs
- Usage accounting
- Cost accounting
- Structured errors
- Streaming support
- Secret masking
- Health monitoring

Verify IVX Owner AI, all 9 agents and all application AI features through the new gateway.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — MIGRATE APIs AND FUNCTIONS
==================================================

Move every Vercel API and function to IVX-controlled backend services.

For each route:

1. Reproduce current behavior.
2. Create replacement route.
3. Add authorization.
4. Add validation.
5. Add rate limits.
6. Add logs and trace IDs.
7. Add tests.
8. Compare old and new responses.
9. Deploy to staging.
10. Verify in staging.
11. Prepare production cutover.

No route may disappear during migration.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — MOBILE AND WEB MIGRATION
==================================================

Update:

- Android
- iOS
- Expo
- Web
- Owner portal
- Admin dashboard
- Public landing page

Replace:

- API base URLs
- AI Gateway URLs
- Upload URLs
- Callback URLs
- Redirect URLs
- WebSocket URLs
- Environment-variable references
- Vercel SDKs
- Vercel-specific fetch logic

Add one centralized runtime configuration module.

Do not leave duplicate production endpoints.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — SECRET AND ENVIRONMENT MIGRATION
==================================================

Inventory and migrate all required environment variables.

For every secret record:

- Old variable name
- New variable name
- Runtime service
- Environment
- Binding status
- Authentication test
- Rotation required
- Evidence

Never display secret values.

Credentials are assumed to exist in approved secret storage.

Only request owner action after runtime verification proves a credential is missing, expired, revoked, invalid, or returns 401/403.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — INFRASTRUCTURE DEPLOYMENT
==================================================

Deploy:

- Production backend
- Staging backend
- AI Gateway
- Redis
- Workers
- Queues
- Monitoring
- Health checks
- Autoscaling
- Rollback target

Verify:

- Multi-instance compatibility
- Shared state
- Session independence
- Worker retries
- Dead-letter handling
- No local-memory dependency for critical shared state
- Zero-downtime deployment

==================================================
PHASE 8 — DNS AND TRAFFIC CUTOVER
==================================================

Use progressive cutover:

Stage 1 — 5% IVX replacement traffic
Stage 2 — 25%
Stage 3 — 50%
Stage 4 — 75%
Stage 5 — 100%

At every stage measure:

- Requests
- Error rate
- p50 latency
- p95 latency
- p99 latency
- Authentication failures
- Chat failures
- AI failures
- Upload failures
- Database errors
- Cost
- Rollback readiness

Automatically roll back if:

- Error rate exceeds 1%
- Critical authentication fails
- Message loss occurs
- Data corruption occurs
- AI Gateway becomes unavailable
- p95 exceeds the approved threshold
- Production health fails

==================================================
PHASE 9 — TESTING
==================================================

Required automated tests:

- AI Gateway tests
- Authentication tests
- API route tests
- Web tests
- Android tests
- Database tests
- Realtime tests
- Upload tests
- Worker and queue tests
- Failover tests
- Security tests
- Regression tests
- Load tests

Required migration tests:

- Old response versus new response
- Session compatibility
- Existing user compatibility
- Existing chat-history compatibility
- Media URL compatibility
- Owner login
- Admin login
- Member login
- Investor login
- AI response
- Realtime messaging
- Push notifications
- File upload
- Payment/financial routes where applicable

==================================================
PHASE 10 — VERCEL-ZERO VERIFICATION
==================================================

After cutover, scan all active source code, build artifacts, deployment settings and runtime logs.

These production scans must return zero active dependencies:

- ai-gateway.vercel.sh
- vercel.app
- vercel.sh
- @vercel/
- VERCEL_
- Vercel webhooks
- Vercel deployment hooks
- Vercel API tokens
- Vercel project IDs
- Vercel runtime imports
- Vercel production DNS
- Vercel production traffic

Historical documentation may be retained only when clearly marked:

HISTORICAL — NOT ACTIVE

==================================================
PHASE 11 — APK AND APPLICATION RELEASE
==================================================

Generate a new Android APK after migration.

Do not reuse an older build.

Required:

- New versionCode
- Git commit embedded
- New API base URL embedded
- New IVX AI Gateway URL embedded
- Zero active Vercel URLs
- SHA-256 checksum
- Signing verification
- Directly downloadable APK attachment or public HTTPS link

Verify the actual APK bundle, not only source files.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — LIVE EVIDENCE LEDGER
==================================================

Every task must store:

- Task ID
- AI agent
- Role
- Start time
- End time
- Files changed
- Tests executed
- Test output
- Commit SHA
- Pull request
- Deployment ID
- Production URL
- Health result
- Trace ID
- Before/after evidence
- Rollback target
- Final status

No evidence means NOT VERIFIED.

==================================================
PHASE 13 — DASHBOARD DETAIL PAGES
==================================================

Create these tabs:

1. Executive Summary
2. 9 AI Live Work
3. Vercel Dependency Inventory
4. Architecture Map
5. API Migration
6. AI Gateway Migration
7. Environment and Secrets
8. Infrastructure
9. DNS and Traffic
10. Tests
11. Deployments
12. Incidents
13. Costs
14. Evidence Ledger
15. Final Certification

Add filters for:

- AI agent
- Module
- Dependency type
- Risk
- Status
- Date
- Failed
- Blocked
- Deployed
- Production verified

==================================================
PHASE 14 — OWNER CONTROLS
==================================================

Owner-only controls:

- Pause migration
- Resume migration
- Approve production cutover
- Trigger rollback
- Freeze deployments
- Reassign task
- Reject evidence
- Open task details
- Download final report
- Download APK
- View secrets status without viewing values
- View production comparison
- View costs
- View active incidents

Dangerous operations require owner confirmation and MFA.

==================================================
FINAL ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
==================================================

The migration is complete only when:

1. All Vercel dependencies are inventoried.
2. Every active Vercel dependency has a replacement.
3. The IVX AI Gateway no longer routes through Vercel.
4. All APIs run on IVX-controlled infrastructure.
5. Mobile and web contain no active Vercel endpoint.
6. Secrets are migrated securely.
7. DNS routes production traffic away from Vercel.
8. Production traffic is 100% IVX infrastructure.
9. Zero active Vercel dependencies remain.
10. Automated tests pass.
11. Load and failover tests pass.
12. Production health remains stable.
13. The rollback path is verified.
14. The new APK is generated.
15. The APK is directly downloadable.
16. The dashboard shows all 9 AI agents and their live evidence.
17. GitHub HEAD equals deployed SHA equals /version SHA.
18. Every completion claim includes evidence.
19. No secrets are exposed.
20. Owner approves final cutover.

==================================================
FINAL REPORT
==================================================

Return:

- Migration completion percentage
- Dependencies discovered
- Dependencies removed
- Dependencies remaining
- Current production architecture
- Current traffic routing
- AI Gateway status
- APIs migrated
- Tests passed and failed
- Commit SHA
- Deployment ID
- Production health
- Rollback target
- New APK filename
- Direct APK download URL
- Estimated monthly cost before and after
- Remaining risks
- Final status

Allowed final statuses:

- DISCOVERY IN PROGRESS
- MIGRATION IN PROGRESS
- STAGING VERIFIED
- PRODUCTION CUTOVER IN PROGRESS
- VERCEL REMOVED — OWNER VALIDATION PENDING
- VERCEL REMOVED — PRODUCTION VERIFIED

Do not mark VERCEL REMOVED until production runtime, traffic, mobile bundles, DNS and active configuration all show zero Vercel dependency.

Start with the dependency inventory and create the live dashboard before modifying production.
